title: quercus × maroccana
intro: Quercus × maroccana, also known as Maroccan cork oak or cork oak hybrid, is a hybrid plant species that is a cross between the Mediterranean cork oak (Quercus suber) and the English oak (Quercus robur). Quercus × maroccana has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been shown to help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Quercus × maroccana include its potential to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer, as well as its ability to support immune function and improve cognitive health. Quercus × maroccana is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it may also be used in herbal supplements or infused into oil for topical application.
